transcript.clean_text: we head to Melbourne for the fifth and final stage of the Jayco herald-sun tour with Joe Henley ten seconds to the good of set Berwick in the overall standings after its impressive win on Mount Buller yesterday it would take a large dose of misfortune in this 89 kilometers II gang groves in Alberto Daisy will be looking to add to their tallies today the likely sprint finish on the cards after 22 circuits around the city streets no prizes for guessing who made the first attack of the day Bridge lanes Ben Hill was once again on the offensive he had a little bit more work to do to mathematically ensure he would keep hold of that green points jersey joining him in the move with some big hitters including James Whelan alex cataford and michael freiburg also in here with jesse youit and jay vine who is sitting very close together in the overall standings at fifth and sixth respectively Hill sprung away at the first intermediate sprint after 20 kilometers I think what cramp could stop him from taking maximum points but in second came Jay vine which saw him provisionally leapfrog Hewitt into fifth place on GC then he'll quit the break soon after that point but there were a couple of other riders keen on replacing him Mark O'Brien of the corner meant the national team escaped on Anderson streets the steepest part of the circuit also in the hunt was re Scott of black spoke the chase was on but they were stuck in no-man's land on this fast circuit in between the brake and the peloton which was now being led by team bridge Ling J vine caught his GT rival yet napping at the second intermediate sprint to give him more of a time buffer on his fifth place overall with under 30 kilometers to go that lead to the main Bunch wasn't really moving above 35 seconds but with three laps to go they hadn't lost much time either Jimmy Whelan pumping up the crowd at the finish line some Webb were in control of the situation though and the last of the s Capiz were brought back with three kilometers to go Tom Scully was making a big effort for ear Pro Cycling on the final time of Anderson Street to try and deliver Moreno hofland to the win the Dutchman was safely on the wheel but over the top he decided to slot in further back as they negotiated the last few corners to war line Caden Grove started his sprint when Scully finally peeled off and it wasn't long before the young Ozzy was up to speed he'd mastered the sloping track to the line and took his second win of the tour in fine style teammate dion smith had time to sit up celebrate and still finish second which tells you all you need to know about how dominant groves his wind was the right hand barrier was the place to be groves leading from the front and never letting go here is your top ten on the stage and just be on the podium more consistent performances from michael rice of ära and mick our rhyme of israel start-up nation Alberto died nazy was caught out of position in the finale winding up 50 from the day congratulations though to Joy Henley of team some Webb he didn't put a foot wrong all week and showed his class on the summit finishes to take Tuesday twins as well said Berwick is on the wrong side of a split and lost a bit of time today but not enough to relinquish his second place with housing in third powerless and fourth and vine securing that top five thanks to those intermediate sprints where next four said Berwick judging by previous winners that the young riders classification at this race may be the world tour in the not-too-distant future we'll wait and see thanks to those two mountain top wins Henley also takes the king of the mountains prize well Ben Hill got his reward being in the break every single day the green points Jersey that's a wrap on the Jayco herald-sun tour thank you very much for watching the live streams and the highlights as well stay tuned to GCN racing next week for the conclusion of the cyclocross season as well as the tour de la Provence
